rulings.Case Title: GREGORY YOUNG ET AL VS. OLIVIA NAVA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Franklin Dawson Beattie, Iii And Piyatida Srikumpol Beattie For Leave To File Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 3, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT FRANKLIN BEATTIE, III, PIYATIDA BEATTIE Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Franklin Dawson Beattie, Iii And Piyatida Srikumpol Beattie For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is continued to May 6, 2021 on Court's own motion. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANE ROULLAND VS. KATY STEADMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 3, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT KATY STEADMAN, MARCI BENNETT DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint properly states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under state and local laws. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that rent increase notices are required to be attached to the complaint. SFRO 37.9(n) is not effective until July 1, 2021 per TPA 2020. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
